query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell:

## Bottom Line

**Foreigners remain aggressive net sellers, with selling accelerating to record daily levels in late June 2026.**

### Most recent figures (late June 2026)
- **June 29 (latest session):** Foreign net selling of **~7.7–7.76 trillion won** — the largest single-day sell-off since September 2001, marking the **7th consecutive selling day**. Domestic investors absorbed it (individuals +4.60tn, institutions +2.93tn).
- **June 26:** KOSPI crashed 5.81% (5th circuit breaker of the year) on heavy foreign/institutional selling.
- **Weekly cumulative (June 19–26):** Foreigners net sold **over 20 trillion won**.

### Trend context
- **May 2026:** All-time record monthly net selling of **44.71 trillion won** (~$30bn) on KOSPI; FSS reported >47tn across all stocks. Foreigners simultaneously bought a record 2.84tn net on KOSDAQ.
- **YTD by early June:** >103 trillion won net sold (record).
- **Pattern:** Heavy selling early May–June 11 → brief net buying June 12–16 (+1.7tn on June 12) → resumed and sharply accelerated June 19–29.
- **Drivers:** Profit-taking after KOSPI's ~108% YTD surge, global semiconductor correction, Samsung/SK Hynix weakness, OpenAI IPO delay news, geopolitical tensions. Won at 1,545 (highest since 2009 crisis).

### For the July 6–10 forecast week
Key catalysts land just before: Korea June trade data (July 1), US jobs report (July 2), and Samsung Q2 preliminary results (early July). Analysts project a wide KOSPI range of 8,400–9,500, with record individual investor cash deposits (~136tn won) cushioning declines.

**Caveat:** Late-June daily figures vary across reports (916bn intraday to 7.7–8.3tn full-day), reflecting intraday accumulation and possibly combined cash+derivatives measures — KRX official cash equity figures are authoritative. Note I did not assign any probability or forecast; this is the current data trajectory only.
